skill.name: nestjs-expert
skill.description: Expert guidance for NestJS application architecture, dependency injection, middleware, guards, interceptors, testing, database integration, and authentication troubleshooting.
skill.summary: Improve NestJS architecture, testing, dependency injection, database setup, and authentication workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many command, URL, JWT secret, and environment-file references. Review found these are primarily Markdown examples, official documentation links, and legitimate NestJS troubleshooting guidance, not malicious behavior. The skill is low risk because it may guide an assistant to run local one-shot diagnostics in a user project.

low_findings.title: Local Diagnostic Commands in Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill includes one-shot shell commands for detecting NestJS setup and validating builds. These commands inspect project files and run package scripts, so users should review them before execution, but they are not evidence of malicious intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible Markdown examples and are scoped to NestJS diagnostics, linting, builds, and tests. I found no hidden command construction, obfuscation, or exfiltration target.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Example URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to NestJS documentation, related tool documentation, community repositories, and one example API value. They do not send data or instruct credential transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are presented as resource links or static example configuration. No evidence found of network calls, webhook endpoints, or data exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: Environment Secret Troubleshooting References
low_findings.description: The skill discusses JWT_SECRET and .env files while explaining common NestJS authentication failures. This is sensitive-topic guidance, but it does not request secrets or direct the assistant to expose them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines advise checking configuration presence and consistency. No evidence found of secret collection, printing secret values, or transmitting environment data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Heuristic Dismissed After Context Review
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer combined command examples, documentation links, and secret-related terminology into a critical heuristic. Context review found benign NestJS troubleshooting content rather than code execution with credential exfiltration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious categories are present, but they occur in separate instructional sections with benign purpose. I found no semantic link that combines commands, network access, and credentials into a harmful workflow.

content.value_statement: NestJS teams often lose time debugging module boundaries, dependency injection, and test setup. This skill gives focused guidance for architecture, validation, database integration, and Passport authentication.
actual_capabilities: Diagnoses NestJS module, provider, and dependency injection issues.
Guides controller, middleware, guard, interceptor, and pipe implementation.
Helps design Jest, Supertest, integration, and e2e test strategies.
Reviews TypeORM, Mongoose, and Prisma integration patterns.
Troubleshoots Passport.js and JWT authentication configuration.
Suggests validation order for builds, unit tests, and e2e tests.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a full automated migration service.
It may suggest local diagnostic commands that users should approve first.
It does not replace security review for production authentication systems.
It depends on project files and package scripts being available.

use_cases.title: Debug Dependency Injection Failures
use_cases.description: Find missing providers, incorrect exports, and circular dependency problems in NestJS modules.

use_cases.title: Plan Reliable Test Coverage
use_cases.description: Create focused unit, integration, and e2e test plans using Jest and Supertest.

use_cases.title: Review Authentication Design
use_cases.description: Check Passport.js, JWT, guards, and configuration patterns before production release.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Module Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my NestJS module structure and explain any missing providers, exports, or imports that could cause dependency injection errors.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Test Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me debug this NestJS Jest or Supertest failure. Focus on module setup, mocks, async handling, and validation order.

prompt_templates.title: Design Authentication Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my NestJS Passport and JWT authentication design. Check strategy setup, guards, token configuration, and environment validation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this NestJS service for module boundaries, database integration, error handling, performance risks, and test coverage gaps.

output_examples.input: A service fails with a dependency resolution error.
output: Identifies the missing constructor dependency position.
Checks provider registration and module exports.
Recommends a minimal module change and validation steps.

output_examples.input: An e2e test cannot start the application module.
output: Reviews test module imports and mocks.
Suggests replacing external services with focused providers.
Orders validation through build, unit tests, and e2e tests.
Checks strategy imports, secret configuration, and bearer token format.
Reviews guard placement and module registration.
Suggests safe configuration validation without exposing secrets.

best_practices: Start with project structure and module boundaries before changing code.
Run typecheck and unit tests before broader integration or e2e tests.
Keep authentication secrets validated but never printed or shared.
anti_patterns: Using forwardRef as the first solution for every circular dependency.
Importing large production modules into isolated unit tests.
Debugging JWT issues by exposing secret values in logs or prompts.

faq.question: What projects is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for NestJS applications that use TypeScript, modules, providers, controllers, tests, databases, or Passport authentication.

faq.question: Can it fix dependency injection errors?
faq.answer: It can diagnose common causes and suggest focused changes to providers, imports, exports, and module boundaries.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: The skill contains diagnostic command examples. Users should approve local command execution in their environment.

faq.question: Can it help with TypeORM and Mongoose?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for connection setup, entity or schema registration, repositories, and testing patterns.

faq.question: Does it handle authentication issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Passport.js, JWT strategies, guards, token format, and safe configuration checks.

faq.question: Is it safe for production secrets?
faq.answer: It should not receive secret values. Share configuration shape and error messages without exposing credentials.
